description.task: Implement a DataStructureBenchmark class that times insertion and lookup operations for three structures: int[] (array), ArrayList<Integer>, and HashMap<Integer,Integer>. For each structure, insert n elements then perform n lookups and print which is fastest.
instructions: timeHashMapInsert(n): record System.nanoTime() start, put n key-value pairs (i -> i) into a new HashMap, return elapsed nanos
timeHashMapLookup(n): build a HashMap with n entries first (outside timing), then time n get(i) calls
Both timing methods must use System.nanoTime() for start and return nanoTime()-start
The benchmark() method is already implemented — only the two TODO methods need code
Actual output values vary by machine; the format must match exactly
description.hint: timeHashMapInsert: long start = System.nanoTime(); Map<Integer,Integer> map = new HashMap<>(); for(int i=0;i<n;i++) map.put(i,i); return System.nanoTime()-start;
description.expectedOutput:
=== n=1000 ===
Insert | Array: [varies]ns
Insert | ArrayList: [varies]ns
Insert | HashMap: [varies]ns
Lookup | Array: [varies]ns
Lookup | HashMap: [varies]ns

description.task: Implement a StructureSelector class with a select(String useCase) method that returns the most appropriate Java collection type as a string. Handle the following use cases: 'undo-stack', 'job-queue', 'word-count', 'ranked-scores', 'ordered-list', 'unique-emails'.
instructions: Implement select() using a switch or a series of if-else checks on the useCase string
Return 'Unknown' for any unrecognized use case
All comparisons should be done with exact lowercase strings as shown
description.hint: Use a switch(useCase) statement with case 'undo-stack': return 'Stack'; etc., and a default: return 'Unknown';
description.expectedOutput:
undo-stack -> Stack
job-queue -> LinkedList
word-count -> HashMap
ranked-scores -> TreeMap
ordered-list -> ArrayList
unique-emails -> HashSet

description.task: Create a TradeoffAnalyzer class that represents complexity trade-offs for common operations across data structures. Store lookup, insert, and delete complexities for Array, LinkedList, HashMap, and TreeMap. Implement a printTradeoffs() method and a bestForOperation(String op) method.
instructions: Add HashMap entry to the static block: complexities.put('HashMap', new String[]{"O(1)", "O(1)", "O(1)"})
Add TreeMap entry: complexities.put('TreeMap', new String[]{"O(log n)", "O(log n)", "O(log n)"})
Do not modify printTradeoffs() or bestForOperation() — they are already implemented
The table must print 4 rows (Array, LinkedList, HashMap, TreeMap) in insertion order
bestForOperation('lookup') returns 'Array' (first O(1) lookup), bestForOperation('insert') returns 'LinkedList'
description.hint: Just add two put() calls inside the static { } block after the LinkedList entry.
description.expectedOutput:
Structure    Lookup     Insert     Delete    
Array        O(1)       O(n)       O(n)      
LinkedList   O(n)       O(1)       O(1)      
HashMap      O(1)       O(1)       O(1)      
TreeMap      O(log n)   O(log n)   O(log n)  
Best for lookup: Array
Best for insert: LinkedList
